subject: AP Psychology
theme: The nervous system coordinates all behavior and mental processes through electrochemical signals passed between billions of specialized cells.
core_concepts: Neurons are the basic building blocks of the nervous system — each consists of a cell body, dendrites, and an axon that transmits signals.
Resting membrane potential is approximately $-70$ mV; action potentials fire when the threshold of approximately $-55$ mV is crossed.
Neurotransmitters are released into the synapse and bind to receptor sites on the postsynaptic neuron, either exciting or inhibiting it.
The central nervous system (brain + spinal cord) processes information; the peripheral nervous system connects it to the rest of the body.
The brain's four lobes — frontal, parietal, temporal, and occipital — each specialize in distinct cognitive and sensory functions.
Neuroplasticity allows the brain to reorganize synaptic connections in response to experience, learning, and injury throughout the lifespan.

key_terms.definition: A nerve cell that receives and transmits electrical and chemical signals throughout the body.
An electrical impulse that travels down the axon when the neuron's membrane potential reaches threshold ($-55$ mV).
The junction between two neurons where neurotransmitters are released from the presynaptic terminal.
A chemical messenger released across a synapse to excite or inhibit the postsynaptic neuron (e.g., dopamine, serotonin).
A fatty layer of insulation around an axon that speeds up action potential transmission via saltatory conduction.
The outer layer of the brain responsible for complex thought, voluntary movement, language, and sensory processing.
The brain's ability to change and adapt structurally and functionally in response to experience.
A brief period after an action potential during which the neuron cannot fire again, ensuring one-way signal transmission.

exam_tip: FRQ questions often ask you to apply brain region knowledge to a case study — always name the specific lobe or structure and explain its function in the context of the scenario described.
